changelog: Universal run-stamp output-file convention. Every fresh-each-run output FILE is now named with a {YYMMDD-HHMM} run stamp (date +%y%m%d-%H%M), computed once per command run and reused verbatim, so a same-day re-run never clobbers a prior run's output. Applied to: all /review:* reports + /review:sweep (each phase shares one stamp; resume reuses the in-progress stamp) + the --council workspace dir; /research + /brainstorm (research/{stamp}-{slug}.md); /investigate (investigations/{stamp}-{slug}.md); /do + /run-todo plans (plans/{stamp}-{do|run-todo}-{slug}.md, with a compute-once note so the write-time name matches the later git add). organize.cjs's already-organized sentinel now recognizes the YYMMDD-HHMM prefix alongside YYYY-MM-DD so stamped flat plans are not relocated out from under the command's git add (+ regression test). Deliberate exceptions documented in the new CLAUDE.md 'Run-Stamp Convention (universal)' section: append-style day logs (intake/triage/agent-updates/memories-daily, which append ## Run HH:MM inside the file), per-day dirs whose inner files already carry time/slug (work/, screenshots/, canary/, pending-curation/, skill-evolution/), predictable resume-state files (onboard-scan, new-project-interview), epic-keyed files (retro-{epic-slug}.md), and in-content date display values. Motivated by a downstream feedback report flagging same-day /review:sweep report collisions.
